event: Christ in the House of Mary and Martha
symbols: Mary seated at Jesus's feet — the contemplative life, choosing 'the better part' that will not be taken away
Martha standing and serving — the active life of service, anxious about many things
Jesus gesturing in teaching — the Word made flesh speaking the words of eternal life
interior domestic space — the ordinary home transformed by Christ's presence into sacred ground
description: An interior scene with figures gathered around a table covered with a red cloth. At center, a figure in a light garment gestures while teaching, turning toward a woman seated at his feet on the lower right, who looks up attentively in a blue and gold dress. Another woman stands to the left near the table, appearing to manage household tasks. Other figures — older men — sit or lean at the table in discussion. Through a window or doorway in the background, more figures are visible in an outdoor courtyard. The lighting is warm and focuses on the central teaching moment.
theological_description: Luke records that Martha 'was distracted by all the preparations that had to be made,' while Mary 'sat at the Lord's feet listening to what he said' (Luke 10:39-40). When Martha complained, Jesus replied: 'Martha, Martha, you are worried and upset about many things, but few things are needed — or indeed only one. Mary has chosen what is better, and it will not be taken away from her' (Luke 10:41-42). This episode has been foundational for the Christian distinction between the contemplative and active lives. Jesus does not condemn Martha's service but reorders priorities — the Word of God takes precedence over even the most generous hospitality. This teaching echoes Deuteronomy: 'Man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord' (Deuteronomy 8:3). The scene in Bethany anticipates Jesus's later declaration: 'My food is to do the will of him who sent me' (John 4:34).

scripture_refs.reason: The narrative of Jesus in the home of Mary and Martha — the direct scene depicted
Man does not live by bread alone but by every word of God — Mary choosing the word over service
Jesus's food is to do the Father's will — spiritual nourishment over physical provision
